iteration_2_phase2.note: conceded hud-fidelity 8->9 on column-arithmetic edit re-read

a_suffix_implementable_no_producer_chang.evidence: index.tsx:269 already sorts logs by `b.ts - a.ts`. The `·Nm` suffix is `Date.now() - sortedLogs[0].ts` formatted with a 5-branch duration switch (s/m/h/d). `entry.ts` is the existing numeric timestamp field on every log entry — no producer-side schema change needed. Section §Sparkline contract row `Empty states` defines the formatter inline (`·45s`, `·2m`, `·15m`, `·1h`, `·3d`).

b_freshest_gutter_override_single_line.evidence: §Verb-glyph column rule 1 contains a labelled paragraph `Gutter override (rule (a))`: 'when rule 1 fires on the freshest row, suppress the gutter `●` from column 0 for that row only.' Single condition, single override, restated unambiguously in §State 4 bullet `Top row freshest+failed has no `●` gutter`. The State 4 mock body itself omits the `●` on row 0 (` ✗ src/index.tsx`), corroborating the rule visually.

c_multithread_tiebreaker_buildable.evidence: §Source position `Tie-breaker — same-agent multi-thread collision` paragraph: 'compare `source[i].slice(0,2)` against `source[i+1].slice(0,2)`; equality is the collision signal.' This is a pairwise scan over the visible window (≤7 rows), explicitly O(n). Resolution: 'fall through to eyebrow mode for *both* rows.' The previous chip-row's source is inspectable because the chip-mode predicate already iterates with index access (run-length classifier in §Rules 1–2).

d_glyph_palette_six_of_six.evidence: §Glyph palette table still enumerates exactly 6 entries (gutter `nf-md-record` + 5 verbs). The new `·Nm` suffix is explicitly called out as `**No codepoint added to the glyph palette** — purely textual` (using U+00B7 + ASCII digits + s/m/h/d). VERIFIED: U+00B7 is ALREADY shipped at index.tsx:282 (`SEPARATOR = " \u00b7 "`), so the spec's EAW-Ambiguous hedge is overcautious — the codebase's terminal-policy assumption is already locked.

e_no_iter2_buildability_regressions.evidence: All three iter-2 buildability fixes preserved verbatim: (1) §Source position `Rule 0 — system tags (highest precedence)` paragraph intact; (2) §Verb-glyph column rule 1 `Bridge to legacy splitOutcome()` paragraph intact, citing index.tsx:210 (verified — splitOutcome lives at index.tsx:210); (3) §States `Column arithmetic` paragraph intact, with `pad(1) | content(28) | pad(1)` ruler convention preserved. No new sections, no new glyphs, no new producer requirements.

hud_fidelity.rationale: Three iter-3 deltas are all hud-positive: (1) `·Nm` suffix only renders in case (ii) where the shape carries no rate — defensible data-ink, not chrome restating; (2) `Gutter override (rule (a))` removes a cell-level double-encoding on freshest+failed rows; (3) tie-breaker preserves small-multiples discipline of the chip column. Holds at 9 rather than pushing to 10 because the `·Nm` text annotation, however well-defended, is text-not-glyph on what is otherwise a pure-glyph instrument row, and the 4-cell footprint is real chrome on the case-(ii) line. Same integer tier as iter-2 but more defensible inside that tier.

four_questions.rationale: This is the iteration's headline fix. Iter-2 sat at 8 because Pattern was 3-of-4 — the flat-sparkline window-empty case conflated wedged (recent burst then 64s silence) with idle (never emitted). Iter-3 closes it: case (i) renders flat alone, case (ii) renders flat + `·Nm`. The binary 'is there a suffix?' is genuinely peripheral — wedged-vs-idle is now glanceable. Doesn't reach 10 because the *value* of N (45s vs 15m vs 3d) requires reading to discriminate degrees of stuck-ness; the binary disambiguation glances, the magnitude reads.

terminal_feasibility.rationale: Net-zero feasibility risk added. Verifications (a)–(e) all pass. The `·Nm` formatter is ~10 lines of duration-switch code; sorting is already in place; gutter override is one boolean predicate (`isFreshest && col1HasSeverityGlyph`); tie-breaker is a pairwise O(n) scan. The U+00B7 codepoint precedent at index.tsx:282 makes the suffix safer than the spec admits. Holds at 9 (not 10) for two minor blemishes: (i) §Source position claims '+1 row per collision pair' but a colliding 2-row pair becomes 4 lines under single-row-eyebrow fallback (eyebrow + content × 2), so the true cost is +2 rows per pair; (ii) the tie-breaker is specified pairwise but doesn't explicitly address chains of 3+ same-agent threads (transitive cascade is implicit but not stated). Both are descriptive slips, not buildability blockers.

asi_focus_for_phase2.single_refinement: Correct the chip-collision row-cost arithmetic in §Source position from '+1 row per collision pair' to '+2 rows per collision pair (each affected row gets its own 1-row eyebrow above it, doubling the pair's vertical footprint from 2 → 4 lines)'.
asi_focus_for_phase2.why_this_one: Three candidate ASIs surfaced during verification: (1) the row-cost arithmetic slip in the tie-breaker; (2) extending the freshest-row gutter override to also fire when rule-0 system-tag glyphs occupy column 1 (currently only rule-1 errors are covered); (3) committing `·` vs ASCII `.` as the primary form (moot — U+00B7 already ships at index.tsx:282). Option 3 is already de-facto resolved by the codebase. Option 2 is a real edge case but rare (system-tag-on-freshest is uncommon and the visual collision is mild). Option 1 is the only outright incorrect claim in iter-3's new content and directly affects vertical-budget planning at the 5-7-row cap, where +1 vs +2 rows per collision pair changes whether a single collision can push older context off-screen. One-line fix; high signal-to-noise.
asi_focus_for_phase2.scope: Single sentence edit in the §Source position `Tie-breaker` paragraph. Optionally, a parallel one-clause note that pairwise detection cascades transitively over 3+ same-agent runs (each adjacent pair triggers fall-through, so a triple becomes three eyebrows). No section additions, no glyph changes, no scope creep — fits the surgical-refinement mandate.

summary: Iter-3 lands its headline four-questions fix (stuck-vs-idle via case-(ii) `·Nm` suffix) cleanly, with two scope-clean side-fixes (freshest+failed gutter override, same-agent chip tie-breaker) that are each individually buildable from existing index.tsx data. All five verification gates (a–e) pass. `·` is already shipped at index.tsx:282 so the EAW hedge is moot. The only descriptive blemish is the tie-breaker's '+1 row per collision pair' claim — the true cost is +2 rows. Provisional 9/9/9 = 9.0 (four-questions 8→9, hud-fidelity and feasibility hold at 9). ASI: correct the row-cost arithmetic in §Source position.
